commands.repair_reason: - [P2] The remaining action is maintainer acceptance of the compatibility-sensitive migration behavior, not a mechanical repair.; Cleared: The diff changes state-migration logic, tests, and comment/lint guardrails only; it does not add dependencies, scripts, permissions, or secret-handling paths. (sha=10a6055e4cd2905fafbc4e2eab0d4619d90b54e3)
commands.review_summary:
This PR changes doctor legacy state migration handling so conflicting legacy plugin install JSON is archived as `.migrated` after SQLite metadata remains canonical, with regression coverage and two guardrail comment/baseline cleanups.

PR surface: Source -5, Tests +4. Total -1 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main returns a warning before `archiveLegacyInstalledPluginIndex`, and the linked issue plus PR body proof show repeated warnings on later commands; I did not execute commands because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining action is maintainer acceptance of the compatibility-sensitive migration behavior, not a mechanical repair.

target.title: fix(doctor): archive conflicting plugin install index
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: legacy `plugins/installs.json` stayed in place when shared SQLite plugin install metadata conflicted with the legacy JSON source, so every later command could re-enter the state migration path and repeat the same warning.
- Solution: keep shared SQLite metadata canonical for conflicting legacy records, then archive the legacy JSON source with the existing `.migrated` flow so the migration reaches a non-repeating end state.
- What changed: updated `src/infra/state-migrations.ts` and the doctor migration regression coverage in `src/commands/doctor-state-migrations.test.ts`; also refreshed two current CI guardrail baselines in `packages/plugin-sdk/src/testing.ts` and `src/plugins/host-hook-runtime.ts` that were failing on the rebased PR branch.
- What did NOT change: no plugin install metadata merge semantics changed for missing records, matching richer records, invalid JSON, or plugin-state SQLite sidecar migrations; the plugin SDK testing barrel still exports the same helper module, and plugin run-context runtime behavior is unchanged.

Fixes #90213

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** Repeated legacy state migration warnings after `openclaw doctor --fix` or later OpenClaw commands when `plugins/installs.json` conflicts with canonical shared SQLite plugin install metadata.
- **Real environment tested:** Local Linux checkout using the real OpenClaw state migration implementation, real shared SQLite installed-plugin index store, and a temporary `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` containing conflicting `brave` and `lobster` install metadata like the reporter evidence.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  ```bash
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/commands/doctor-state-migrations.test.ts src/infra/state-migrations.state-dir.test.ts src/cli/program/config-guard.test.ts
  OPENCLAW_REPO_ROOT="$TASK_WORKTREE" PROOF_RUN_LABEL=patched-pr-head-10a6055e4c node --import tsx "$EVIDENCE_DIR/repeating-plugin-index-warning-proof.ts"
  corepack pnpm@11.2.2 build
  OPENCLAW_REPO_ROOT="$TASK_WORKTREE" PROOF_RUN_LABEL=doctor-cli-built-pr-head-10a6055e4c node --import tsx "$EVIDENCE_DIR/doctor-fix-cli-proof.ts"
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.contracts-plugin.config.ts src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-package-contract-guardrails.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/run-context-lifecycle.contract.test.ts
  corepack pnpm@11.2.2 lint --threads=8
  git diff --check
  ```
- **Evidence after fix:**
  Latest main before the patch still repeats the warning and leaves the legacy source in place:
  ```text
  PROOF_RUN_LABEL=latest-main-fffd72f36d
  FIRST_WARNINGS=["Left plugin install index in place because shared SQLite state has conflicting plugin install metadata for: brave, lobster"]
  FIRST_CHANGES=[]
  LEGACY_SOURCE_EXISTS_AFTER_FIRST=true
  LEGACY_ARCHIVE_EXISTS_AFTER_FIRST=false
  SECOND_WARNINGS=["Left plugin install index in place because shared SQLite state has conflicting plugin install metadata for: brave, lobster"]
  SECOND_CHANGES=[]
  CANONICAL_BRAVE_SPEC=brave@latest
  CANONICAL_LOBSTER_SPEC=lobster@latest
  ```

  Patched PR head archives the legacy source on the first run and the second run is quiet:
  ```text
  PROOF_RUN_LABEL=patched-pr-head-10a6055e4c
  FIRST_WARNINGS=[]
  FIRST_CHANGES=["Kept shared SQLite plugin install metadata for conflicting legacy records: brave, lobster","Archived plugin install index legacy source → /tmp/openclaw-90213-proof-VLmlXi/plugins/installs.json.migrated"]
  LEGACY_SOURCE_EXISTS_AFTER_FIRST=false
  LEGACY_ARCHIVE_EXISTS_AFTER_FIRST=true
  SECOND_WARNINGS=[]
  SECOND_CHANGES=[]
  CANONICAL_BRAVE_SPEC=brave@latest
  CANONICAL_LOBSTER_SPEC=lobster@latest
  ```

  Built CLI launcher proof with `openclaw.mjs doctor --fix` reaches the same non-repeating state:
  ```text
  PROOF_RUN_LABEL=doctor-cli-built-pr-head-10a6055e4c
  FIRST_DOCTOR_EXIT=0
  FIRST_DOCTOR_HAS_REPEATED_WARNING=false
  LEGACY_SOURCE_EXISTS_AFTER_FIRST_DOCTOR=false
  LEGACY_ARCHIVE_EXISTS_AFTER_FIRST_DOCTOR=true
  SECOND_DOCTOR_EXIT=0
  SECOND_DOCTOR_HAS_REPEATED_WARNING=false
  CANONICAL_BRAVE_SPEC=brave@latest
  CANONICAL_LOBSTER_SPEC=lobster@latest
  ```

  Regression tests and checks:
  ```text
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/commands/doctor-state-migrations.test.ts src/infra/state-migrations.state-dir.test.ts src/cli/program/config-guard.test.ts
  [test] passed 3 Vitest shards in 114.54s

  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.contracts-plugin.config.ts src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-package-contract-guardrails.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/run-context-lifecycle.contract.test.ts
  Test Files 3 passed (3)
  Tests 91 passed (91)

  corepack pnpm@11.2.2 build
  [build-all] phase timings: total 707.7s

  corepack pnpm@11.2.2 lint --threads=8
  [oxlint:core] finished
  [oxlint:extensions] finished
  [oxlint:scripts] finished

  git diff --check
  # no output
  ```
- **Observed result after fix:** Conflicting legacy plugin install JSON is preserved as `plugins/installs.json.migrated`, shared SQLite remains canonical for `brave` and `lobster`, and a second migration run emits no warnings or changes.
- **What was not tested:** No globally installed npm package, live PiClaw upgrade, real Brave/Lobster plugin activation, external Brave Search API calls, LM Studio, or provider/model traffic was exercised.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level: state migration unit/integration coverage plus real source-runtime proof with a temporary state directory.
- Target test file: `src/commands/doctor-state-migrations.test.ts`.
- Scenario locked in: conflicting same-version npm plugin install records now archive `plugins/installs.json` and a follow-up migration run has no warnings or changes.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: it exercises the same doctor migration entry point and persisted SQLite index store that produce the repeated warning without needing external plugins or credentials.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: `migrateLegacyInstalledPluginIndex` returned immediately when legacy JSON conflicted with existing shared SQLite install metadata, so it skipped the existing archive step and left `plugins/installs.json` available for every later migration scan.
- Missing detection / guardrail: the old regression test asserted that the legacy source stayed in place, but did not assert the required non-repeating post-repair state.
